description: A lightweight CLI tool written in TypeScript that fetches and prints the latest stable n8n release version from the npm registry.

userStories.title: Initialize TypeScript project with CLI entry point
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the project scaffolded with package.json, tsconfig.json, and a bin entry point so that the CLI can be built and linked globally.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json exists with name 'n8nrel' and bin field mapping 'n8nrel' to the compiled entry point
tsconfig.json is configured to compile TypeScript to a dist/ directory
A src/index.ts file exists with a shebang line (#!/usr/bin/env node)
Running 'npm run build' compiles the project without errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fetch and print the latest stable n8n version
userStories.description: As an n8n self-hoster, I want to run 'n8nrel' in my terminal so that I can see the latest stable n8n version without opening a browser.
acceptanceCriteria: Running 'n8nrel' makes an HTTP GET request to https://registry.npmjs.org/n8n/latest
The version field from the JSON response is printed to stdout followed by a newline (e.g. '2.6.4')
The tool exits with code 0 on success
If the network request fails or the response is malformed, a human-readable error message is printed to stderr and the tool exits with a non-zero exit code
No external HTTP libraries are used (use Node.js built-in fetch)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Install as a global CLI command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run 'npm link' so that the 'n8nrel' command is available globally in my macOS terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: After running 'npm run build && npm link', typing 'n8nrel' in a new terminal session executes the tool
The compiled output runs with Node.js without requiring ts-node or other dev dependencies at runtime
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Support --beta and --next flags for non-stable versions
userStories.description: As an n8n self-hoster, I want to run 'n8nrel --beta' or 'n8nrel --next' so that I can check the latest beta or next n8n version.
acceptanceCriteria: Running 'n8nrel --beta' fetches https://registry.npmjs.org/n8n/beta and prints the version to stdout
Running 'n8nrel --next' fetches https://registry.npmjs.org/n8n/next and prints the version to stdout
Running 'n8nrel' without flags still prints the latest stable version (default behavior unchanged)
If both --beta and --next are provided, the tool prints an error to stderr and exits with a non-zero exit code
If an unknown flag is provided, the tool prints a usage hint to stderr and exits with a non-zero exit code
Typecheck passes
